abstract: Decay properties of $^{194,195,196}\mathrm{Pb}$ were studied using singles and coincident $\ensuremath{\gamma}$-ray spectroscopy. Sources were produced by the 95 MeV $^{197}\mathrm{Au}$($^{6}\mathrm{Li}$,$\mathrm{xn}$) reactions. No direct evidence for the decay of a low-spin isomer of $^{195}\mathrm{Pb}$ was observed. Several new levels were observed in $^{195,196}\mathrm{Tl}$, and are interpreted in terms of a rotational model.RADIOACTIVITY $^{194,195,196}\mathrm{Pb}$ [from $^{197}\mathrm{Au}$($^{6}\mathrm{Li}$,$\mathrm{xn}$), $E=95$ MeV]. Measured ${E}_{\ensuremath{\gamma}}$, ${I}_{\ensuremath{\gamma}}$, $\ensuremath{\gamma}\ensuremath{-}\ensuremath{\gamma}$ coin; deduced $log\mathrm{ft}$. $^{194,195,196}\mathrm{Pb}^{m}$ deduced ${t}_{\frac{1}{2}}$. $^{194,195,196}\mathrm{Tl}$ deduced levels, $J$, $\ensuremath{\pi}$, Nilsson configurations. Ge(Li) detectors.
